[38;5;252m[1m## Implementation Plan: Tenant Document Upload via S3[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Current State[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- **Upload handler** ([38;5;10mhandlers/documentos.rs[0m) already handles multipart form parsing for any [38;5;10mentity_type[0m (including [38;5;10minquilino[0m).[0m[0m
- **Service** ([38;5;10mservices/documentos.rs::upload[0m) writes files to local filesystem using [38;5;10mUPLOAD_DIR[0m.[0m[0m
- **No AWS SDK dependency** exists in the project today.[0m[0m
- The [38;5;10mDocumento[0m entity already has [38;5;10mfile_path[0m (currently stores a relative local path like [38;5;10minquilino/{id}/{uuid}-filename.pdf[0m).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### What Needs to Change[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 1. Add [38;5;10maws-sdk-s3[0m dependency[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Add to [38;5;10mbackend/Cargo.toml[0m:[0m[0m
[1mtoml
[0m[38;5;10maws-sdk-s3 = "1.82.0"   # verify latest
aws-config = "1.6.3"     # verify latest
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 2. Create an S3 storage module[0m[0m
[0m[0m
New file: [38;5;10mbackend/src/services/storage.rs[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Responsibilities:[0m[0m
- Initialize [38;5;10mS3Client[0m from environment ([38;5;10mAWS_REGION[0m, [38;5;10mS3_BUCKET[0m, optional [38;5;10mS3_ENDPOINT[0m for MinIO/local dev).[0m[0m
- [38;5;10mupload_object(bucket, key, data, content_type) -> Result<String, AppError>[0m — PUTs bytes to S3 and returns the key.[0m[0m
- [38;5;10mgenerate_presigned_url(bucket, key, expiry_secs) -> Result<String, AppError>[0m — for serving downloads without exposing the bucket directly.[0m[0m
- [38;5;10mdelete_object(bucket, key) -> Result<(), AppError>[0m — for the existing [38;5;10meliminar[0m handler.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The S3 client should be built once at startup (in [38;5;10mapp.rs[0m) and injected as [38;5;10mweb::Data<S3StorageClient>[0m.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 3. Modify [38;5;10mservices/documentos.rs::upload[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Replace the [38;5;10mtokio::task::spawn_blocking[0m filesystem write with:[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10mlet key = format!("{org_id}/{entity_type}/{entity_id}/{file_uuid}-{safe_filename}");
s3_client.upload_object(&key, file_data, mime_type).await?;
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Store [38;5;10mkey[0m in [38;5;10mdocumento.file_path[0m (same column, now holds the S3 object key instead of a local path).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 4. Modify the download/serve path[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Currently files are served by reading from disk. Replace with presigned URL generation or streaming from S3. Two options:[0m[0m
- **Option A (recommended):** Return a short-lived presigned GET URL (e.g., 15 min) — shifts bandwidth off the backend.[0m[0m
- **Option B:** Stream from S3 through the backend — useful if access control needs per-request enforcement beyond the signed URL.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 5. Modify [38;5;10meliminar[0m handler[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Call [38;5;10ms3_client.delete_object(&doc.file_path)[0m instead of [38;5;10mstd::fs::remove_file[0m.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 6. Configuration[0m[0m
[0m[0m
New env vars:[0m[0m
| Variable | Purpose | Example |[0m[0m
|---|---|---|[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mS3_BUCKET[0m | Bucket name | [38;5;10mrealestate-docs[0m |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mS3_REGION[0m | AWS region | [38;5;10mus-east-1[0m |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mS3_ENDPOINT[0m | Override for MinIO/local | [38;5;10mhttp://minio:9000[0m |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mSTORAGE_BACKEND[0m | [38;5;10ms3[0m or [38;5;10mlocal[0m (graceful migration) | [38;5;10ms3[0m |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m#### 7. Tenant-specific usage[0m[0m
[0m[0m
No handler changes needed — the existing upload endpoint already accepts [38;5;10mentity_type = "inquilino"[0m in the path ([38;5;10m/api/{entity_type}/{entity_id}/documentos[0m). Tenants' documents are already linked via the polymorphic [38;5;10mDocumento[0m entity.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Migration Strategy[0m[0m
[0m[0m
1. Add a [38;5;10mSTORAGE_BACKEND[0m env var. When [38;5;10mlocal[0m, keep current behavior. When [38;5;10ms3[0m, use the new S3 path. This allows deploying without a flag day.[0m[0m
2. Existing documents with local [38;5;10mfile_path[0m values can be migrated in bulk (one-time script to upload local files to S3 and update [38;5;10mfile_path[0m in the DB).[0m[0m
3. Alternatively, detect at read time: if [38;5;10mfile_path[0m starts with [38;5;10mhttp[0m or matches a known S3 key pattern, use S3; otherwise fall back to local.[0m[0m

|---|---|[0m[0m

| [38;5;10mbackend/src/services/storage.rs[0m | [1mNew[22m — S3 client wrapper |[0m[0m

| [38;5;10mbackend/src/services/documentos.rs[0m | Replace filesystem write/delete with [38;5;10mstorage[0m calls |[0m[0m

[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Security Considerations[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- S3 bucket must be private (no public access). All reads go through presigned URLs or backend proxy.[0m[0m
- Object keys are namespaced by [38;5;10morganizacion_id[0m to prevent cross-tenant access.[0m[0m
- The existing [38;5;10mverificar_entidad_pertenece_a_org[0m check already guards uploads.[0m[0m
- Presigned URLs should have short TTLs (15 min max).[0m[0m
- File validation (magic bytes, size limit, mime allowlist) stays as-is — it already runs before upload.[0m[0m

[0m[0m

[0m[0m
[1mcsv
[0m[38;5;10mcontrato_id,monto,moneda,fecha_vencimiento,fecha_pago,metodo_pago,notas

[0m[0m

[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Endpoint[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;10mPOST /api/pagos/importar-csv

[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Processing Strategy: Two-phase (validate all → insert all)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mPhase 1 — Parse & Validate every row:[22m[0m[0m
1. Parse CSV, reject on structural errors (missing columns, bad types)[0m[0m
2. Per row:[0m[0m
   - Validate [38;5;10mmonto > 0[0m, [38;5;10mmoneda ∈ {DOP, USD}[0m, [38;5;10mmetodo_pago[0m if present[0m[0m
   - Verify [38;5;10mcontrato_id[0m exists in the org (single batch query for all unique contrato_ids)[0m[0m
   - Verify each contrato has [38;5;10mestado = activo[0m (or optionally [38;5;10mvencido[0m for late payments)[0m[0m
3. Collect all row errors with line numbers[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mPhase 2 — Bulk insert in a single transaction:[22m[0m[0m
- Only executes if Phase 1 produced zero errors[0m[0m
- Wraps all inserts in one DB transaction[0m[0m
- Runs the same ITBIS/cuota logic as the existing [38;5;10mpagos::create[0m per record[0m[0m
- Audits the batch as a single action[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Response Shape[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mjson
[0m[38;5;10m// Success

[0m[0m
| Check | Rationale |[0m[0m
|---|---|[0m[0m
| contrato exists in org | Tenant isolation / IDOR prevention |[0m[0m
| contrato.estado = [38;5;10mactivo[0m | Can't record payments for cancelled/terminated contracts |[0m[0m
| No duplicate [38;5;10m(contrato_id, fecha_vencimiento)[0m within the CSV | Prevent accidental double-import |[0m[0m

[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Size Limits[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Max file size: 1 MB (configurable)[0m[0m
- Max rows: 500 per import (prevents timeout, keeps transaction reasonable)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Why this approach[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- **All-or-nothing**: either the entire file imports or nothing does — no partial state to debug[0m[0m
- **Batch contrato lookup**: one [38;5;10mSELECT ... WHERE id IN (...)[0m instead of N queries[0m[0m

[38;5;252m[1m## Schema Design: [38;5;10mpagos_recurrentes[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The system already has [38;5;10mpago_generacion.rs[0m which calculates monthly pagos from a contrato's dates. What's missing is a [1mconfigurable recurring rule[22m that a background job reads to auto-generate pagos each month, independent of manually triggering it per contrato.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Table: [38;5;10mpagos_recurrentes[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1msql
[0m[38;5;10mCREATE TABLE pagos_recurrentes (
    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organizacion_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizaciones(id),
    contrato_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES contratos(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    monto           DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
    moneda          VARCHAR(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DOP',
    dia_vencimiento INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (dia_vencimiento BETWEEN 1 AND 31),
    activo          BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    proxima_generacion DATE NOT NULL,  -- next month to generate a pago for
    ultima_generacion  DATE,           -- last fecha_vencimiento successfully generated
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT uq_pago_recurrente_contrato UNIQUE (contrato_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pagos_recurrentes_proxima ON pagos_recurrentes (proxima_generacion)
    WHERE activo = TRUE;
CREATE INDEX idx_pagos_recurrentes_org ON pagos_recurrentes (organizacion_id);
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### How it works[0m[0m
[0m[0m
| Column | Purpose |[0m[0m
|--------|---------|[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mcontrato_id[0m (unique) | One recurring rule per contrato. Prevents double-generation. |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mmonto[0m / [38;5;10mmoneda[0m | Copied from contrato at creation but can be overridden (e.g., annual adjustments). |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mdia_vencimiento[0m | Which day of the month the pago's [38;5;10mfecha_vencimiento[0m lands on (clamped to month end for short months, same logic as existing [38;5;10mcalcular_pagos[0m). |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mactivo[0m | Set to [38;5;10mFALSE[0m when contrato is cancelled/terminated/expired. |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mproxima_generacion[0m | The month (as first-of-month or the vencimiento date) for which the next pago should be generated. Advances after each run. |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10multima_generacion[0m | Audit trail — the last fecha_vencimiento that was emitted. |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Generation flow (background job)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
A scheduled task (using the existing [38;5;10mejecuciones_tareas[0m tracking) runs daily/hourly:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1msql
[0m[38;5;10mSELECT pr.*
FROM pagos_recurrentes pr
JOIN contratos c ON c.id = pr.contrato_id
WHERE pr.activo = TRUE
  AND c.estado = 'activo'
  AND pr.proxima_generacion <= CURRENT_DATE;
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
For each row:[0m[0m
1. Insert a [38;5;10mpago[0m with [38;5;10mestado = 'pendiente'[0m, [38;5;10mfecha_vencimiento = proxima_generacion[0m (clamped day), [38;5;10mmonto[0m, [38;5;10mmoneda[0m, [38;5;10mcontrato_id[0m.[0m[0m
2. Update [38;5;10multima_generacion = proxima_generacion[0m.[0m[0m
3. Advance [38;5;10mproxima_generacion[0m by 1 month.[0m[0m
4. If [38;5;10mproxima_generacion > contrato.fecha_fin[0m, set [38;5;10mactivo = FALSE[0m.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Lifecycle hooks[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- **Contrato created** → Insert a [38;5;10mpagos_recurrentes[0m row with [38;5;10mproxima_generacion[0m = first month based on [38;5;10mfecha_inicio[0m + [38;5;10mdia_vencimiento[0m (default to day 1 or contrato's existing logic).[0m[0m
- **Contrato cancelled/terminated** → Set [38;5;10mactivo = FALSE[0m.[0m[0m
- **Contrato renewed** (new fecha_fin) → Update [38;5;10mactivo = TRUE[0m if needed, keep [38;5;10mproxima_generacion[0m advancing from where it left off.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Why not just use contratos directly?[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- **Decoupling**: Allows overriding [38;5;10mmonto[0m (rent increases mid-contract) without altering the contrato's [38;5;10mmonto_mensual[0m.[0m[0m
- **Idempotency**: [38;5;10mproxima_generacion[0m acts as a cursor — the job can crash and restart safely without duplicating pagos.[0m[0m
- **Pause/resume**: Setting [38;5;10mactivo = FALSE[0m stops generation without cancelling the contrato (e.g., tenant temporarily abroad).[0m[0m
- **Audit**: [38;5;10multima_generacion[0m gives visibility into what was last emitted.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Consistency with existing system[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Reuses the existing [38;5;10mcalcular_pagos[0m day-clamping logic for [38;5;10mdia_vencimiento[0m.[0m[0m
- Generated pagos are identical in structure to manually-created ones — same [38;5;10mpagos[0m table, same states.[0m[0m
- The [38;5;10mejecuciones_tareas[0m table already tracks background job runs with success/failure and affected records.[0m[0m
- The unique constraint on [38;5;10mcontrato_id[0m prevents the "overlapping active contracts" invariant from being violated at the generation level.

[38;5;252m[1m## Pagos Handler Review[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Auth Guards ✅ Correct[0m[0m
[0m[0m
| Endpoint | Extractor | Correct? |[0m[0m
|----------|-----------|----------|[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mlist[0m | [38;5;10mClaims[0m (any authenticated user) | ✅ Read-only, appropriate |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mget_by_id[0m | [38;5;10mClaims[0m | ✅ Read-only |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mcreate[0m | [38;5;10mWriteAccess[0m | ✅ Requires admin/gerente |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mupdate[0m | [38;5;10mWriteAccess[0m | ✅ |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mbulk_marcar_pagado[0m | [38;5;10mWriteAccess[0m | ✅ |[0m[0m
| [38;5;10mdelete[0m | [38;5;10mAdminOnly[0m | ✅ Most restrictive for destructive op |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
All endpoints also scope by [38;5;10morganizacion_id[0m from claims — no IDOR risk.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Error Handling ✅ Solid[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- All service calls use [38;5;10m?[0m propagation through [38;5;10mAppError[0m.[0m[0m
- State transitions validated before mutation ([38;5;10mvalidate_transition[0m).[0m[0m
- [38;5;10mbulk_marcar_pagado[0m verifies all IDs exist and are in updatable state before any write.[0m[0m
- Transactions used for all write operations so partial failures roll back.[0m[0m
- Audit logging is best-effort (non-blocking), so it doesn't mask errors.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Currency Handling ⚠️ One Issue[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mProblem in [38;5;10mcreate[0m (service layer, line ~170):[22m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10mmoneda: Set(input.moneda.unwrap_or_else(|| "DOP".to_string())),
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
When [38;5;10mmoneda[0m is [38;5;10mNone[0m in the request, the service defaults to [38;5;10m"DOP"[0m. Per the product invariant:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;244m│ **Currency consistency**: every monetary entity carries its own [38;5;10mmoneda[0m field. Never assume currency from context.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The [38;5;10mCreatePagoRequest[0m makes [38;5;10mmoneda[0m optional ([38;5;10mOption<String>[0m), and the service silently fills in DOP. This violates the "never assume currency" rule.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mFix:[22m Make [38;5;10mmoneda[0m required in [38;5;10mCreatePagoRequest[0m, or inherit it from the referenced contrato's [38;5;10mmoneda[0m field (which is already fetched in that function as [38;5;10mcontrato_model[0m).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The safer fix — inherit from the contrato (since a pago logically shares the contrato's currency):[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10m// In services/pagos.rs create():
moneda: Set(input.moneda.unwrap_or_else(|| contrato_model.moneda.clone())),
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Or better yet, make it non-optional on the DTO and validate it matches the contrato:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10m// In models/pago.rs:
pub moneda: String,  // not Option<String>
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mSame issue in [38;5;10mregistrar_pago_parcial[0m:[22m It uses [38;5;10mcontrato_model.moneda.clone()[0m directly — this is correct since the partial payment inherits the contrato's currency explicitly.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Minor Observations[0m[0m
[0m[0m
1. [1m[38;5;10mvalidate_create_pago[0m in the handler validates [38;5;10mmoneda[0m only if present[22m — if you keep it optional, the handler validation doesn't catch missing values, only invalid ones.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
2. [1m[38;5;10mUpdatePagoRequest[0m has no [38;5;10mmoneda[0m field[22m — correct; you shouldn't be able to change a payment's currency after creation.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
3. [1mMetric tracking uses the fallback [38;5;10m"DOP"[0m too[22m — if you fix the defaulting above, this resolves itself.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### Summary[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The code is well-structured with proper auth guards, org-scoping, transactional writes, and state machine validation. The one actionable issue is the implicit DOP default in [38;5;10mcreate[0m — the currency should either be required on the DTO or explicitly inherited from the contrato rather than silently assumed.

[1mSummary of changes:[22m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
| What | Before | After | Why |[0m[0m
|------|--------|-------|-----|[0m[0m
| DB pool [38;5;10mmax_connections[0m | 25 | 10 | Each connection holds ~5-10MB of buffers under load. With HPA (2-5 replicas), 10 per pod = 20-50 total connections — plenty for this workload. |[0m[0m
| DB pool [38;5;10mmin_connections[0m | 5 | 2 | Reduces idle memory footprint |[0m[0m
| Actix workers | default (all node cores) | 2 | With a 1 CPU limit, >2 workers just causes contention and inflates RSS. 2 workers fully saturate 1 core. |[0m[0m
| Memory request | 512Mi | 512Mi | Unchanged — keeps scheduling fair |[0m[0m
| Memory limit | 768Mi | 1Gi | Gives headroom for request body buffering (file uploads) and connection pool growth under burst traffic |[0m[0m
| CPU request | 200m | 250m | Slightly higher priority in scheduling since we're now explicitly using 2 workers |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
The HPA already handles horizontal scaling (2→5 pods at 70% CPU), so each pod doesn't need to be over-provisioned — it's better to scale out than scale up.

input: What is the current API for SeaORM's find_with_related and how does it differ from load_many for batch loading?
stdout:
Running tool [38;5;141mresolve-library-id[0m with the param[38;5;244m (from mcp server: context7)[0m
 ⋮  {
 ⋮    "query": "find_with_related vs load_many batch loading related entities",
 ⋮    "libraryName": "SeaORM"
 ⋮  }
[0m[38;5;244m - Completed in 0.929s[0m

Running tool [38;5;141mquery-docs[0m with the param[38;5;244m (from mcp server: context7)[0m
 ⋮  {
 ⋮    "libraryId": "/websites/rs_sea-orm_2_0_0-rc_34",
 ⋮    "query": "find_with_related vs load_many batch loading related entities"
 ⋮  }
[0m[38;5;244m - Completed in 1.377s[0m

Running tool [38;5;141mquery-docs[0m with the param[38;5;244m (from mcp server: context7)[0m
 ⋮  {
 ⋮    "libraryId": "/websites/rs_sea-orm_2_0_0-rc_34",
 ⋮    "query": "find_with_related SQL JOIN eager loading"
 ⋮  }
[0m[38;5;244m - Completed in 2.812s[0m

[38;5;141m> [0mHere's the current API (as of SeaORM 2.0-rc) and how the two approaches differ:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## [38;5;10mfind_with_related[0m — JOIN-based eager loading[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10mlet cake_with_fruits: Vec<(cake::Model, Vec<fruit::Model>)> = Cake::find()
    .find_with_related(Filling)
    .all(db)
    .await?;
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Executes a single SQL query with a [38;5;10mLEFT JOIN[0m[0m[0m
- Returns [38;5;10mVec<(ParentModel, Vec<RelatedModel>)>[0m — rows are automatically consolidated by the left entity[0m[0m
- Works for both 1-N and M-N relations (M-N does two joins through the junction table)[0m[0m
- Variant: [38;5;10mfind_also_related[0m returns [38;5;10mVec<(Model, Option<RelatedModel>)>[0m for 1-1 relations[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## [38;5;10mload_many[0m (LoaderTrait) — separate-query batch loading[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10mlet cakes: Vec<cake::Model> = Cake::find().all(db).await?;

let fruits: Vec<Vec<fruit::Model>> = cakes
    .load_many(fruit::Entity::find(), &db)
    .await?;
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Executes a [1mseparate[22m query for the related entities (2 queries total instead of 1 JOIN)[0m[0m
- Called on a [38;5;10mVec<Model>[0m (implements [38;5;10mLoaderTrait[0m)[0m[0m
- Returns [38;5;10mVec<Vec<R::Model>>[0m — index-aligned with the input vec, so [38;5;10mfruits[i][0m are the fruits for [38;5;10mcakes[i][0m[0m[0m
- Siblings: [38;5;10mload_one[0m (has_one → [38;5;10mVec<Option<R::Model>>[0m), [38;5;10mload_many_to_many[0m (M-N with junction entity)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## Key differences[0m[0m
[0m[0m
| | [38;5;10mfind_with_related[0m | [38;5;10mload_many[0m |[0m[0m
|---|---|---|[0m[0m
| SQL strategy | Single query with JOIN | 2 separate queries |[0m[0m
| Return type | [38;5;10mVec<(Parent, Vec<Child>)>[0m | [38;5;10mVec<Vec<Child>>[0m (parallel to parent vec) |[0m[0m
| Data duplication | Parent columns repeated per child row | No duplication |[0m[0m
| Composability | Must be chained from a [38;5;10mSelect[0m | Can be called on any [38;5;10mVec<Model>[0m you already have |[0m[0m
| Multiple relations | Need separate queries anyway | Natural — call [38;5;10mload_many[0m / [38;5;10mload_one[0m for each relation independently |[0m[0m
| Filtering children | Filters apply to the whole joined set | Can pass a filtered [38;5;10mEntity::find().filter(...)[0m as the stmt |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mWhen to use which:[22m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- **[38;5;10mfind_with_related[0m** — when you need one relation loaded and want a single round-trip, or need to filter/sort based on joined columns.[0m[0m
- **[38;5;10mload_many[0m** — when you need to load multiple different relations for the same parent set, want to avoid row duplication for wide parent tables, or already have the parent models in hand. This is the recommended approach for batch loading in SeaORM 2.0 since it avoids the N+1 problem without JOIN bloat.

[38;5;252m[1m## Recommended Crates for Actix-web 4 Rate Limiting[0m[0m
[0m[0m
There are three solid options, each with different tradeoffs:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### 1. [38;5;10mactix-governor[0m (most popular, simplest)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Backed by the [[38;5;10mgovernor[0m](https://github.com/antifuchs/governor) crate (GCRA algorithm — a smooth, cell-rate–based limiter). 130 stars, well-maintained. [1mGPL-3.0 licensed[22m — check if that's compatible with your project.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mtoml
[0m[38;5;10m[dependencies]
actix-governor = "0.6"
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10muse actix_governor::{Governor, GovernorConfigBuilder};
use actix_web::{web, App, HttpServer};

#[actix_web::main]
async fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let governor_conf = GovernorConfigBuilder::default()
        .requests_per_second(2)
        .burst_size(5)
        .finish()
        .unwrap();

    HttpServer::new(move || {
        App::new()
            .wrap(Governor::new(&governor_conf))
            .route("/", web::get().to(|| async { "Hello" }))
    })
    .bind("127.0.0.1:8080")?
    .run()
    .await
}
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Pros: zero config for basic use, in-memory by default, supports key extraction (IP, headers, custom).[0m[0m
Cons: GPL-3.0 license, no Redis backend built-in.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### 2. [38;5;10mactix-extensible-rate-limit[0m (most flexible, MIT/Apache)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Fixed-window algorithm with pluggable backends (in-memory via DashMap, or Redis). 1,850 downloads/month. Latest: 0.4.0 (Aug 2024).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mtoml
[0m[38;5;10m[dependencies]
actix-extensible-rate-limit = "0.4"
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10muse actix_extensible_rate_limit::{
    backend::{memory::InMemoryBackend, SimpleInputFunctionBuilder},
    RateLimiter,
};
use actix_web::{App, HttpServer};
use std::time::Duration;

#[actix_web::main]
async fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let backend = InMemoryBackend::builder().build();

    HttpServer::new(move || {
        let input = SimpleInputFunctionBuilder::new(Duration::from_secs(60), 5)
            .real_ip_key()
            .build();
        let middleware = RateLimiter::builder(backend.clone(), input)
            .add_headers()
            .build();
        App::new().wrap(middleware)
    })
    .bind("127.0.0.1:8080")?
    .run()
    .await
}
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Pros: MIT/Apache license, custom key extractors, dynamic limits per-request, custom 429 responses, [38;5;10mx-ratelimit-*[0m headers, Redis support, rollback on certain response codes.[0m[0m
Cons: fixed-window only (no token bucket/sliding window).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m### 3. [38;5;10mbrakes[0m (multi-algorithm, distributed)[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Supports fixed window, sliding window, token bucket, and leaky bucket. Backends: memory, Redis, Memcached. Latest: 0.1.1 (Jan 2025). MIT/Apache licensed.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mtoml
[0m[38;5;10m[dependencies]
brakes = { version = "0.1.1", features = ["actixweb"] }
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10muse actix_web::{App, HttpServer, web, get, Responder, HttpResponse};
use brakes::{
    backend::local::Memory,
    middleware::actixweb::ActixwebRateLimiter,
    types::token_bucket::TokenBucket,
    RateLimiter,
};
use std::time::Duration;

#[get("/")]
async fn hello() -> impl Responder {
    HttpResponse::Ok().body("Hello")
}

#[actix_web::main]
async fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let limiter = RateLimiter::builder()
        .with_backend(Memory::new())
        .with_limiter(TokenBucket::new(5, Duration::from_secs(10)))
        .build();
    let middleware = ActixwebRateLimiter::new(limiter);

    HttpServer::new(move || {
        App::new()
            .wrap(middleware.clone())
            .service(hello)
    })
    .bind("127.0.0.1:8080")?
    .run()
    .await
}
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Pros: multiple algorithms, distributed backends, custom key extractors and callbacks.[0m[0m
Cons: very new (0.1.x), smaller community.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## Recommendation for this project[0m[0m
[0m[0m
Given your backend is Actix-web 4 with MIT/Apache licensing expectations and you already use Redis (via K8s):[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1m[38;5;10mactix-extensible-rate-limit[0m[22m is the best fit — permissive license, Redis backend for distributed rate limiting across replicas, custom key extraction (you can key by user ID from your JWT claims), and [38;5;10mx-ratelimit-*[0m response headers. It integrates cleanly as a [38;5;10m.wrap()[0m middleware at either the [38;5;10mApp[0m or [38;5;10mweb::scope[0m level, so you can apply different limits to auth endpoints vs. data endpoints.[0m[0m
[0m[0m
If you later need token bucket or sliding window semantics, [38;5;10mbrakes[0m with the Redis feature is the upgrade path.[0m[0m

But if the attacker's request hits [3manother[23m replica (or before [38;5;10minvalidate()[0m propagates in multi-instance deployments), old tokens work for up to 60s from the last cache refresh.[0m[0m
